patient.name: Lillian Graham
symptoms: noticing a bump in palm over time

decision.triage_reasoning: Patient has symptoms consistent with possible trigger finger or Dupuytren's contracture, which while not life-threatening, show signs of moderate symptoms that may require intervention. The mild diabetes adds a level of caution since it can affect overall healing and may complicate treatment.
decision.notes: Symptoms include difficulty straightening the ring finger and tenderness; these warrant further evaluation. No acute distress is noted and vital signs are stable.
decision.context_for_next: Patient will need an orthopedic evaluation for hand symptoms and consideration of treatment options.

decision.recommended_treatment: Option for corticosteroid injection or splinting, depending on severity.
decision.context_for_next: Patient has moderate symptoms consistent with trigger finger, which has been successfully treated in similar cases with corticosteroid injection or splinting.
decision.reasoning: Based on nurse assessment indicating semi-urgent symptoms consistent with trigger finger and four successful similar cases. No new lab tests are necessary, as symptoms are clear and treatment options exist that have previously succeeded.

final_diagnosis.recommended_treatment: Option for corticosteroid injection or splinting, depending on severity.
final_diagnosis.context_for_next: Patient has moderate symptoms consistent with trigger finger, which has been successfully treated in similar cases with corticosteroid injection or splinting.
